dimensions.finding: All substantive HLD claims cite contract refs or SRC evidence, including approved PRDs, adapter local docs, LaunchConfig implementation, OpenClaw official docs, concrete environment, concrete task file, and concrete progress-judgment skill package.
Control flow covers task loading, skill visibility check, adapter launch, initial send, state polling, idle capture, OpenClaw judgment, next-instruction routing, delivery check, and handoff.
Data flow and object design covers TaskDescription, LaunchConfigArtifact, ExecutorRunState, adapter evidence, OpenClaw request and decision, mechanical delivery check, and final output artifacts.

Interfaces include LaunchConfig generation, exact cta commands for launch/capture/status/input/exit, exact OpenClaw local skill install/check commands, OpenClaw agent invocation with --message/--local/--timeout/--json, and reviewer handoff.
Technical decisions preserve local CLI scope, adapter-only Claude control, OpenClaw-only subjective judgment, mechanical checks, evidence preservation, and reviewer-owned semantic acceptance.

Acceptance names concrete task workspace, concrete task file, concrete reference document, concrete local OpenClaw skill package, output directory, command, preconditions, steps, artifacts, checks, failure criteria, evidence, and owner.
Markdown renders the structured HLD with diagrams, tables, source-backed interface precision, exact invocation boundary, executable acceptance design, artifacts, checks, and failure criteria.

The HLD uses source-backed LaunchConfig fields, source-backed cta commands, a concrete local skill package, and official OpenClaw CLI commands; it does not invent adapter flags or a direct skill-run command.
The package contains HLD artifacts only and no task plan, task graph, work items, dependency edges, or parallel groups.

overall_findings: The HLD uses the real ClaudeTmuxAdapter cta CLI and LaunchConfig implementation, ships the local executor-progress-judgment skill package required by OpenClaw, and aligns acceptance task permissions with the actual cta/openclaw commands.
